channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lenovo has put an AI chip into a monitor that not only controls it, but can also give AI powers to your PC
item.description: Lenovo's AI Display is a screen with a discrete NPU inside that can transfer AI powers to your non-AI PC.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: New Sony WH-1000XM6 leak points to an imminent launch, and a choice of 3 colors
item.description: It looks as though the follow-up headphones to the Sony WH-1000XM5 will be arriving sooner rather than later.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Extra cash with no catch? Spotting retiree job scams
item.description: Reports of task scams have significantly risen in the past four years, according to the Federal Trade Commission. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some red flags.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leaked iPhone 17 schematics show the Apple phones could be getting a speaker redesign
item.description: It seems the iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 17 Pro Max could be in line for a modified speaker configuration.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This smart baby monitor with dual mode and enhanced alerts from Momcozy will give new parents peace of mind
item.description: The Momcozy 5-inch Dual-Mode Smart Baby Monitor is a great choice for parents who want to keep an eye on their little ones. It's packed with features including motion and cry detection and temperature monitoring, so you can be certain that your baby is in good hands when monitored by Momcozy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DOGE isn’t Silicon Valley innovation—it’s just a sloppy rebrand of free-market dogma
item.description: At a press conference in the Oval Office earlier this month, Elon Musk—a billionaire who is not, at least formally, the President of the United States—was asked how the Department of Government Efficiency manages potential conflicts of interest to ensure “accountability and transparency.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: How to watch Brit Awards 2025 online from anywhere and for free
item.description: With Jack Whitehall hosting and Sabrina Carpenter, The Last Dinner Party and Sam Fender performing, here’s how you can watch Brit Awards 2025 from anywhere.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alexa+ – Here’s how to sign up for early access
item.description: Amazon's now letting customers in the United States sign up for early access to Alexa+. Here's how to do just that and the Echo Show device you'll need to be eligible.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: East Bay startup’s new stove that plugs into a normal wall outlet could be major gain for health and climate
item.description: The sleek, standard four-burner electric induction stove runs on 120 volts, meaning there is no need to pay a licensed electrician thousands of dollars to rewire to 240 volts, which many electric stoves require.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: How to avoid the hidden costs of onboarding
item.description: For employees, onboarding shapes their first impression of the company, helping them feel welcome and confident in their decision to join. For employers, onboarding involves numerous costs – both monetary and less quantifiable – that can make or break the bottom line.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Singapore arrests three in AI chip fraud case amid US crackdown on exports
item.description: Singapore authorities have charged three men in connection with an alleged fraud scheme involving the illicit procurement of Nvidia’s high-end AI chips. The men, two Singaporeans and one Chinese national, are accused of misrepresenting end-user details to acquire the advanced hardware.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is an AI server? Why artificial intelligence needs specialized systems
item.description: AI servers are advanced computing systems designed to handle complex, resource-intensive AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why fast-learning robots are wearing Meta glasses
item.description: AI and robots need data — lots of it. Companies that have millions of users have an advantage in this data collection, because they can use the data of their customers. A well-known example is Google’s reCAPTCHA v2 service, which I recently lambasted.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China cyber espionage up by 150 percent in 2024: Report
item.description: China's cyber espionage operations continue to become more aggressive amid the increasing use of emerging technologies like artificial intelligence (AI) to help carry out attacks, according to a report published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Long-dormant Mac malware returns with advanced capabilities
item.description: Just two months into 2025 there have been numerous Mac malware threats targeting Apple laptops, ranging from infostealers to malicious software.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Signal will exit Sweden rather than dilute message security
item.description: The CEO of Signal said Tuesday that the service will leave Sweden rather than comply with a rule that will require vendors to capture all secure messages and save a plain text copy, in case authorities later want to subpoena that data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18 expands cloud-native networking
item.description: Red Hat is updating its OpenShift platform with a series of capabilities that will provide more advanced networking and virtualization functionality for cloud-native deployments. OpenShift is Red Hat’s commercially supported Kubernetes distribution.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: I’m a tech expert. These are the 5 time-saving tech tips I tell everyone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson presents five secret shortcuts: Adjust sound/vibration, share YouTube moments, fix tabs and tweak brightness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
